sections.title: A: Use the word given in capitals to form a word
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap.
sections.passage: Frank Turner, the (1) ...... in a trial that has attracted national attention, was today convicted of murder. The police (2) ...... lasted for a year and during the trial over 100 hours of (3) ...... were heard. Turner's (4) ...... had all argued that he was not in the area at the time, but could not provide the necessary (5) ...... . Police described Turner as a well-known (6) ...... who was responsible for many (7) ...... in the local region. This is not Turner's first (8) ...... . Seven years ago, he was found guilty of (9) ...... and served three years in prison. The judge is expected to sentence Turner to a period of (10) ...... later this week.

items.sentence: I heard that they're going to bring **G** in a law banning smoking in public places.
items.answer: G – in a law banning smoking in public places.

items.sentence: Police are appealing for members of the public to come **D** forward with any information they feel might be useful.
items.answer: D – forward with any information they feel might be useful.

items.sentence: Nobody was convinced when the man claimed the gun had gone **A** off accidentally in his hand.
items.answer: A – off accidentally in his hand.

items.sentence: The policewoman started to take **H** down everything I was saying and I knew I was in serious trouble.
items.answer: H – down everything I was saying and I knew I was in serious trouble.

items.sentence: Lots of old people have been taken **C** in by this trick and have lost a lot of money.
items.answer: C – in by this trick and have lost a lot of money.

items.sentence: Peterson was found guilty of holding **E** up the bank and was sentenced to five years in prison.
items.answer: E – up the bank and was sentenced to five years in prison.

items.sentence: Many people get **B** away with serious crimes every day.
items.answer: B – away with serious crimes every day.

items.sentence: The policeman decided to let Shaun **F** off this time, but told him that he wouldn't be so lucky next time.
items.answer: F – off this time, but told him that he wouldn't be so lucky next time.

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
Police said there hadn't been a crime as bad as this in over ten years. **the**
Police described it ...... over ten years.

The laws in some countries are so strict that people have very little freedom. **such**

The witness thought I was the thief, but realised that she was wrong. **for**

Nobody in the country knows the law as well as Mr Parkhurst. **than**

Suddenly, someone shouted, 'Thief!' and the man quickly ...... on a motorbike.
Do you have ...... to take that bicycle?
He said it was an accident, but I know he did it on .......
